tasks.title: Repair participant interest defaults and success path
tasks.objective: Make the cohort detail interest form default to a valid participant user and guide users through showing interest without first hitting creator-only errors.
authority: Preserve current demo-user auth boundary until regular auth is implemented.
Do not weaken the rule that creators cannot show interest in their own cohort.
Prefer UI defaults and guidance over service-level exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: A newly created cohort detail page defaults the interest form to a valid participant, not the creator.
Submitting the default interest form records interest for the demo participant when capacity and credits allow.
If quorum is met, the participant sees the unlocked private link.
verification: Smoke test create -> feed -> detail -> show interest in the browser.
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once the default participant path works and creator rejection is still enforced.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 08:51 EDT: Detail interest form now defaults to a non-creator participant, creator rejection remains tested, and browser smoke verified create -> detail -> show interest -> quorum unlock.

tasks.title: Simplify credit wording and refund messaging
tasks.objective: Replace internal staking and ledger wording with simple user-facing credit copy across MVP pages.
authority: Keep ledger terms in backend code and tests where they describe implementation semantics.
Use simple product language in visible UI.
Add refund reassurance without implying credits return after quorum succeeds.
acceptance_criteria: Create flow says creators use 2 credits to start a cohort.
Interest flow says participants use 1 credit to show interest.
A visible banner or nearby copy says credits are returned if quorum is not met.
User-facing pages no longer rely on 'stake' terminology.
Dashboard copy distinguishes available, in use, used, and returned credits in plain language.
verification: Search rendered UI source for remaining user-facing staking terms.
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once visible MVP copy uses simple credit language and tests reflect the new wording.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 08:51 EDT: UI and README now use plain credit language, refund reassurance is shown on create/feed/detail, and dashboard summaries use available/in use/used/returned wording.

tasks.title: Enforce first meeting after quorum window
tasks.objective: Prevent creators from selecting or submitting first meeting dates before the two-week quorum window has passed.
authority: Interpret the user rule as firstMeetingAt must be after createdAt plus the 14-day expiry/quorum window.
Enforce the rule server-side even if the browser date picker is bypassed.
Expose a date picker minimum in the UI.
acceptance_criteria: Create form first meeting input has a min value based on the earliest allowed date.
Submitting a first meeting before the earliest allowed date is rejected with a clear error.
Submitting a valid first meeting after the two-week window succeeds.
Tests cover both UI min rendering and backend validation.
verification: Run create cohort and domain validation tests.
Browser smoke test that past/too-early dates cannot be accepted.
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once both UI and backend reject dates before the two-week quorum window.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 08:51 EDT: Create form renders a first-meeting min value and backend validation rejects firstMeetingAt at or before createdAt + 14 days.

tasks.title: Clarify MVP navigation and dashboard access
tasks.objective: Make the creator and participant dashboards discoverable and make post-action next steps obvious across the MVP.
authority: Keep demo identity explicit until auth work replaces it.
Avoid adding real account state or auth flows.
Prioritize navigable MVP workflow over marketing-page content.
acceptance_criteria: Primary navigation consistently exposes cohorts, create, creator dashboard, and participant dashboard where appropriate.
Create success state links to the created cohort and creator dashboard.
Interest success state links or guides users to participant dashboard.
Home page presents actual MVP actions rather than implementation-area placeholder cards.
verification: Run dashboard and MVP verification tests.
Browser smoke test home -> create -> feed/detail -> interest -> dashboards.
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once the dashboard routes are discoverable and the MVP end-to-end journey has clear next steps.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 08:51 EDT: Navigation consistently exposes cohorts/create/creator dashboard/participant dashboard; create and interest success states link to next-step dashboards.
